description: DarkMarket was an English-language cybercrime forum created by Renukanth Subramaniam in London. It facilitated the buying and selling of stolen identities and credit card data, with 2,500 users at its peak. The forum was infiltrated by FBI agent J. Keith Mularski, leading to over 60 arrests worldwide. It was shut down in 2008, and Subramaniam was sentenced to nearly five years in prison in 2010.

country_links.evidence_quote: German prosecutors in the cities of Koblenz and Oldenburg said 12 January 2021 that they had shut down what was 'probably the largest illegal marketplace on the Darknet ' called DarkMarket and physically arrested the man believed to operate it near Germany's border with Denmark.
country_links.context: The DarkMarket darknet market was shut down by German prosecutors, who arrested its operator near Germany's border with Denmark.

country_links.evidence_quote: Working with our international partners in the United Kingdom, Germany, and Turkey, the FBI conducted a two-year undercover operation to penetrate the organization and bring it to its knees.
country_links.context: The FBI worked with Turkey as an international partner in the operation against DarkMarket.
